purpose: Record human map-challenge disproof attempts for current 2026 high-overhead, high-ratio, and same-trailhead warning routes. This is not a mathematical global optimum claim; it is the proof ledger for the best route set currently supported by accepted anchors, generated candidate challenges, boundary recombination checks, global optimizer checks, GPX continuity, p75 timing, and DEM effort evidence.

review_frames.question: Can the same official credit be earned from a better known accepted or user-reviewed anchor?
review_frames.result: No deterministic same-credit dominance remains after FD14D, FD03A, FD09A, and H1 repairs.

review_frames.question: Do same-trailhead cards hide an obvious car-to-car bundle that saves human footmiles?
review_frames.result: Warnings remain useful for scheduling, but no bundle is currently proven to reduce on-foot miles while preserving exact credit, p75 constraints, certified GPX, and route-card readiness.

review_frames.question: Do adjacent package boundaries create a better recombination?
review_frames.result: Boundary challenge checkpoints cover the major high-overhead boundaries and found no dominant generated replacement with p75 and DEM effort metrics.

review_frames.question: Does the executable candidate universe contain a dominant whole-plan replacement?
review_frames.result: The global optimizer challenge found zero dominant solutions and does not beat the current route set.

review_frames.question: Would a real hiker reject this route because the extra footmiles are dead mileage rather than necessary single-car access, direction, ascent, or schedule cost?
review_frames.result: The routes below still contain expensive footmiles, but the disproof attempts did not find an actionable replacement that is more field-ready than the current route.

proofs.decision: Attempted disproof by exact-candidate replacement, high-overlap alternative, same-trailhead bundle, and package-boundary recombination. The cheaper high-overlap routes leave target segments behind; the boundary challenge with adjacent Military/Freestone work has no dominant replacement; splitting the old aggregate into the current cards is lower fatigue than reviving the 25.12 mile aggregate.

evidence.alternative_challenge: route-alternative-challenge-ratio-gap reports block-freestone_three_bears_curlew as the current best exact candidate, with no better exact or superset candidate.
evidence.high_overlap_rejection: Shorter high-overlap Freestone candidates miss 3-5 target segments.
evidence.boundary_challenge: route-boundary-challenge-p02-p13 reports no dominant generated combo and generated_combo_beats_current false.
evidence.route_repeat_audit: Freestone same-trailhead warnings remain optimization warnings only; hard repeat failures are zero.

proofs.remaining_caveat: This area still has expensive access and repeat cost; a future field-discovered connector or a new legal start could reopen it.

proofs.decision: Attempted disproof by reviving the old Package 16 all-in-one route, bundling same-start Dry/Sweet routes, and generated exact alternatives. The old Hawkins/Sweet placeholder is materially worse; the accepted 16A split is already integrated; same-trailhead warnings do not yet produce a certified lower-footmile route.

evidence.package16_manual_design: package16-manual-route-design-v1 rejects the 36.48 mile Hawkins placeholder and accepts the 16A split probe.
evidence.current_packet: Current map data includes manual-16a-1 and manual-16a-2 as promoted field-day loops with GPX validation.
evidence.alternative_challenge: route-alternative-challenge-ratio-gap reports connector-highlands-trail-dry-creek-trail has no better exact or superset candidate.
evidence.boundary_challenge: route-boundary-challenge-p06-p15-p16 reports no dominant generated combo.

proofs.remaining_caveat: Parking and wet-weather conditions remain day-of constraints for Dry/Sweet access.

proofs.decision: Attempted disproof by same-trailhead bundle, Polecat/Cartwright recombination, and exact-candidate replacement. The generated Cartwright/Peggy aggregate and Polecat core are already the best exact candidates in the available universe; no proven bundle reduces human footmiles without adding unowned credit or violating route-card readiness.

evidence.alternative_challenge: route-alternative-challenge-ratio-gap reports block-cartwright_peggy_interface and block-polecat_core have no better exact or superset candidate.
evidence.boundary_challenge: route-boundary-challenge-p06-p15-p16 reports no dominant generated combo.
evidence.route_repeat_audit: Cartwright same-trailhead warnings remain optimization warnings only; hard repeat failures are zero.

proofs.remaining_caveat: Polecat direction/signage remains a day-of field gate.

proofs.decision: Attempted disproof by Bogus day-one/day-two recombination, same-lodge bundling, and exact-candidate replacement. The small-credit Bogus cards are high-ratio because official segments sit away from practical lodge parking and often require out-and-back access. The boundary challenge found two metrics that looked better, but no dominant route that beats current without a material tradeoff.

evidence.alternative_challenge: route-alternative-challenge-ratio-gap reports block-bogus_mores_lodge_tempest has no better exact or superset candidate.
evidence.boundary_challenge: route-boundary-challenge-p17-p18 reports generated_combo_beats_current false and dominant_generated_combo_count 0.
evidence.route_repeat_audit: Simplot/Pioneer same-trailhead warnings remain optimization warnings only; hard repeat failures are zero.

proofs.remaining_caveat: Bogus routes remain condition-sensitive; closures, snow, and lodge access require day-of verification.

proofs.decision: Attempted disproof through same-trailhead bundling and the 8th/Crestline superset. The generated superset covering Upper 8th/Corrals/Sidewinder plus Crestline is materially worse than the current split, while Hulls warnings do not yet produce a lower-footmile certified replacement.

evidence.alternative_challenge: route-alternative-challenge-ratio-gap reports block-upper_8th_corrals_sidewinder as current best exact; its generated Crestline superset is worse.
evidence.route_repeat_audit: 8th Street and Hulls same-trailhead warnings remain optimization warnings only; hard repeat failures are zero.

proofs.remaining_caveat: Lower Hulls odd/even and trail-direction constraints still need normal field-day verification.

proofs.area: Table Rock / Old Pen / Castle Rock
proofs.decision: Attempted disproof by replacing the old Table Rock aggregate with shorter high-overlap options and supersets. The current split preserves the official work at lower field cost than the old aggregate; high-overlap alternatives miss target segments and supersets are materially longer.

evidence.alternative_challenge: route-alternative-challenge-ratio-gap reports the Table Rock aggregate has no better exact candidate; shorter high-overlap candidates miss 1-4 segments and supersets are worse.

proofs.remaining_caveat: Table Rock access/signage must still be checked before the outing.

proofs.decision: Attempted disproof by accepted-anchor and exact-credit review. Cervidae is isolated and has no generated replacement; FD14D already moved from stale Full Sail to the lower N 36th accepted anchor; Peace Valley/Harris Ridge has no proven lower-footmile replacement in the current candidate universe.

evidence.cervidae_boundary: route-boundary-challenge-p19 has only one candidate in pool and no dominant generated combo.
evidence.fd14d_route_review: route-review-all-dev passes FD14D after the lower N 36th accepted-anchor replacement.

proofs.remaining_caveat: These remain sensitive to day-of trailhead legality, parking, and condition checks.

proofs.area: West Climb / Harrison / Full Sail loop
proofs.decision: Attempted disproof because the route has high declared repeat mileage. Keep current: the route has a low 1.35 on-foot/official ratio, no hidden self-repeat, no latent credit problem, no unpriced repeat, complete GPX coverage, and no deterministic same-credit dominance finding.

evidence.route_repeat_audit: FD12A repeats are declared and priced; hidden_self_repeat_ids, latent_credit_ids, and unpriced_repeat_ids are empty.
evidence.route_review_gate: route-review-all-dev reports no deterministic same-credit dominance failures.
evidence.efficiency_context: FD12A is not a high-ratio or high-overhead component in the route-efficiency audit.

proofs.remaining_caveat: The repeated official segments make the cue sheet worth reading carefully in the field, but the repeat is accounted for rather than hidden.

limitations: This registry does not prove an imaginary route or brand-new anchor cannot exist.
This registry does not replace day-of closure, legality, mud, heat, or signage checks.
Routes with provisional accepted-replacement status still need their separate field-walkthrough promotion decision before being called fully field-proven.
